claim_text: FY2020 connected fitness product revenue was $1.462B and subscription revenue was $363.7M; product revenue growth was attributed partly to stay-at-home orders during COVID-19.
notes: Official 10-K; used to identify COVID demand-shock contamination.

claim_text: FY2020 ending connected fitness subscriptions were 1.091M, average net monthly churn was 0.62%, total workouts were 164.5M and average monthly workouts per connected fitness subscription were 17.9.
FY2021 connected fitness product revenue was $3.150B, subscription revenue was $872.2M and total revenue was $4.022B.
notes: Peak growth year in backtest period.

claim_text: FY2021 ending connected fitness subscriptions were 2.331M, average net monthly churn was 0.61%, total workouts were 459.7M and average monthly workouts per subscription were 22.0.
notes: Shows real engagement but not normalized future hardware demand.

claim_text: For the three months ended December 31 2021, connected fitness product revenue declined 8.5% year over year and for the six-month period declined 11.8% year over year.
notes: Pre-FY2022 collapse evidence of hardware-demand normalization.

claim_text: Q2 FY2022 connected fitness product gross margin fell to 6.4% from 35.3% in Q2 FY2021, driven by price reduction, logistics deleveraging, component costs and recall charges.
notes: Key early downgrade signal before full-year negative product gross margin.

claim_text: SEC companyfacts show inventory of $937.1M at June 30 2021, $1.541B at December 31 2021, and $1.105B at June 30 2022.
notes: Inventory rose into demand normalization; relevant to capacity/inventory commitment risk.

claim_text: FY2022 connected fitness product revenue fell 30.5% to $2.188B while subscription revenue rose 59.9% to $1.395B; total revenue fell 10.9% to $3.582B.
notes: Shows subscription growth did not prevent total-company decline.

claim_text: FY2022 ending connected fitness subscriptions were 2.966M, average net monthly churn was 0.96%, total workouts were 540.0M and average monthly workouts per subscription fell to 16.4.
notes: Installed base continued growing while engagement per subscription weakened.

claim_text: FY2022 connected fitness products gross margin decreased to negative 11.3% from 29.0% in FY2021, with excess-inventory reserves and logistics costs among cited drivers.
notes: Hard evidence that the hardware acquisition engine broke.

claim_text: FY2022 net loss was $2.828B, operating cash flow was negative $2.020B and free cash flow was negative $2.357B.
SEC companyfacts show goodwill fell from $224.4M at December 31 2021 to $41.2M at March 31 2022.
notes: Supports asset-value reset evidence; not the main downgrade trigger.

claim_text: MacroTrends and StatMuse search results state PTON's all-time high stock closing price was $167.42 on January 13 2021.
notes: Direct MacroTrends page returned 403 during browser open; retain as source-improved but not final archive-grade.

claim_text: CompaniesMarketCap shows PTON year-end market cap of $44.38B in 2020 and $11.80B in 2021.
notes: Useful for market-cap heat and collapse; not exact peak-date EV.

claim_text: StatMuse search result states PTON had market capitalization of $52.8B on December 31 2020 using 347.89M shares at $151.72.
notes: Conflicts with CompaniesMarketCap year-end value; use to show valuation-source variance.

claim_text: PTON Q2 FY2021 10-Q disclosed 294.5M Class A and Class B shares outstanding as of January 29 2021, $1.248B cash/equivalents, $862.1M marketable securities and no drawn credit-facility borrowings as of December 31 2020.
notes: Used to reconstruct approximate near-peak enterprise value.

claim_text: Peloton's investor-relations-hosted Q2 FY2022 transcript records $1.13B Q2 revenue, FY2022 revenue guidance of $3.7-$3.8B, adjusted EBITDA loss of $266.5M, and management discussion of connected-fitness gross-margin pressure from lower volume and fixed-cost deleverage.
notes: Transcript support for the downgrade trigger: hardware-demand normalization and margin pressure were visible before the FY2022 full-year postmortem.

claim_text: Public-source attempts found partial PTON Q1/Q2 FY2022 consensus and guidance-reset context, but not a complete contemporaneous FY2022/FY2023 revenue, EBITDA and EPS consensus package with decision-date provenance.
notes: Use as unavailable-data exception support only; do not label modeled values as consensus.

claim_text: Using the $167.42 all-time high close, 294.5M near-peak shares and Q2 FY2021 filing cash/debt, PTON's approximate peak-era equity value was about $49.3B and approximate near-peak enterprise value was about $47.2B.
notes: Still approximate because price date, share count and cash/debt are near-peak references rather than exact same-day values; contemporaneous consensus gap remains.
